name: Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
avoid: June–November is wetter and overlaps the main Caribbean storm season, so ferry crossings, Tobago Cays day sails, and La Soufrière summit attempts are more likely to be disrupted.
Bequia and Mustique room rates jump sharply around Christmas, New Year, and Easter, so splurge stays often need to be booked months ahead in the dry season.
Saint Vincent minibuses and inter-island ferries are practical but not precision-timed; same-day island hops can feel rushed if seas are rough or sailings shift.
stopoverNote: For trips under 6 days, keep the plan to mainland Saint Vincent plus Bequia, then treat Mustique or Tobago Cays as a single splurge day rather than trying to overnight-hop across every island.

cities.name: Kingstown
cities.notes: The capital is the most practical first base for Fort Charlotte, the Botanical Gardens, market walks, and early departures toward the Rabacca side for La Soufrière hikes.

cities.name: Bequia
cities.notes: Use Port Elizabeth and Admiralty Bay as the easy-going Grenadines base for ferry arrivals, water-taxi beach hops, and day sails toward Mustique or the Tobago Cays.

cities.name: Mustique
cities.notes: Mustique is the polished private-island detour for Macaroni Beach, Britannia Bay, Basil's Bar, and a classic one-day or one-night luxury break between other Grenadines stops.

cities.name: Tobago Cays
cities.notes: The marine park is a boat-access highlight rather than a town; most travelers visit by day cruise and sleep on nearby Bequia, Mayreau, or Canouan after snorkeling with turtles.

itinerary.sim: Digicel and Flow prepaid packs are the simplest options; budget about ₹1,200–₹2,400 for enough maps, WhatsApp, and ferry coordination, and download offline maps before sailing south into the Grenadines.

Kingstown.name: Kingstown
Kingstown.note: Stay near Kingstown, Villa, or the Blue Lagoon side if you want easier airport access, stronger restaurant options, and a cleaner pre-dawn start for the volcano-hike day.

days.theme: Fort views, Botanical Gardens, and the compact capital core

activities.name: Fort Charlotte
activities.tip: The fort is generally visited without an entrance fee; budget separately for a taxi up the hill if you do not want the climb from town.

activities.name: St. Vincent Botanical Gardens
activities.tip: The official non-national adult fee is US$5, roughly EC$13.5 or about ₹420; the optional guided tour is EC$10 extra, about ₹310.

activities.name: Kingstown Market, Bay Street, and harborfront walk
St. George's Cathedral exterior stop

days.theme: La Soufrière summit day and the island's dramatic windward side

activities.name: La Soufrière volcano hike with transport
activities.tip: Published group rates from operators such as Fraser Taxi & Tours and Topdawg Tours commonly land around US$95–100 per adult, or roughly EC$257–270.

activities.name: Rabacca Dry River viewpoint stop
Black Point Tunnel coastal photo stop
Fresh-fruit or roadside bake break on the windward return

Bequia.name: Bequia
Bequia.note: Port Elizabeth is the simplest base because ferries dock here, most taxis and water taxis start here, and you can still reach Princess Margaret Beach or Lower Bay without changing hotels.

days.theme: Ferry arrival, Admiralty Bay orientation, and beach time close to Port Elizabeth

activities.name: Bequia Express or Admiral ferry from Kingstown to Port Elizabeth
activities.tip: The published adult foot-passenger fare is EC$25 one way, with return tickets commonly listed at EC$45 or about ₹1,395.

activities.name: Port Elizabeth harborfront and Belmont Walkway stroll
Princess Margaret Beach swim stop
Admiralty Bay water taxi to Princess Margaret Beach or Lower Bay
activities.tip: Short water-taxi hops are usually negotiated on the spot; expect the fare to vary by sea conditions, departure beach, and whether others are sharing the boat.

days.theme: Quieter bays, plantation views, and a classic slow-Bequia day

activities.name: Lower Bay Beach morning
Firefly Plantation Bequia lunch-and-viewpoint stop
Spring Bay or Industry Bay coastal stop
Devil's Table viewpoint at sunset

Mustique.name: Mustique
Mustique.note: Mustique is best treated as a splurge stop or day sail from Bequia unless you specifically want a luxury overnight at Cotton House or Firefly.

days.theme: Macaroni Beach, Britannia Bay, and the island's iconic lunch stop

activities.name: Friendship Rose Mustique day sail from Bequia
activities.tip: The scheduled Mustique cruise is published at US$150 per adult and is typically sold as a full-day sail rather than a simple ferry transfer.

activities.name: Macaroni Beach
Britannia Bay and Lovell Village walk
Basil's Bar lunch or sunset drinks

meals: The Veranda at The Cotton House

Tobago Cays.name: Tobago Cays
Tobago Cays.note: There are no conventional hotels inside the marine park itself, so use a day boat from Bequia or sleep on nearby Mayreau or Canouan if you want an early start on the water.

days.theme: Marine-park sail, turtle snorkeling, and a Salt Whistle Bay stop

activities.name: Tobago Cays snorkeling day trip from Bequia
activities.tip: Current shared-boat rates from operators such as Octopus, Gusto, and Friendship Rose usually start around US$130 and can reach US$175 including snorkel gear and the park fee.

activities.name: Baradel turtle snorkel
Petit Bateau / Jamesby beach time
Salt Whistle Bay swim stop on nearby Mayreau

connections.method: Bequia Express or Admiral inter-island ferry from the Grenadines wharf to Port Elizabeth (about 1h)
Best handled as a scheduled day sail or arranged private water-taxi/charter rather than a frequent public ferry
connections.cost: ₹12,555 pp on Friendship Rose's scheduled day sail; private charters cost more

connections.method: Full-day catamaran or schooner trip south via Mustique/Canouan/Mayreau, with snorkeling stops built in

extras: Saint Vincent's local minibuses are the cheapest mainland transport and work well for short hops around Kingstown, but they are cash-only, loosely timed, and less useful for pre-dawn volcano starts.
Water taxis are especially handy around Bequia's Admiralty Bay and around Mayreau landing spots; agree the fare before boarding because pricing changes with distance, luggage, and sea state.
December through May is the driest and easiest stretch for La Soufrière, ferry crossings, and Tobago Cays visibility, but it is also when Bequia and Mustique availability gets tightest.
Tobago Cays trips are weather-sensitive even in the dry season; book them early in your Bequia stay so you still have room to rebook if winds or swell pick up.
If you only have one premium splurge day, Tobago Cays usually delivers more value than trying to overnight on Mustique unless the goal is specifically luxury dining and resort downtime.
